steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content:
Before the blank: “The measurement is based on how much and how quickly weight can be moved” is an independent clause.

After the blank: “one unit of mechanical horsepower is equivalent to the amount of power it takes to lift 550 pounds one foot off the ground in one second” is an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses with a transition phrase
steps.content:
When two independent clauses are connected by a transition phrase, use a semicolon before the phrase and a comma after it (IC; for example, IC).

Choice C correctly applies this rule: “moved; for example,” properly joins the clauses while introducing the example.

The other choices fail this pattern—A and B use only a comma (comma splice), while D omits punctuation (run-on).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
